ontology.foodon_gap_note: No specific FoodOn entry for Biscoff-flavoured cream cake. FOODON_00002400 (cake food product) used as closest parent.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A00LC (Cakes and pastries) is the closest defensible category. No specific Biscoff cake entry exists in FoodEx2.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT code identified for this specific commercial dessert cake product.

preparation_method: topped with Biscoff spread and crushed Biscoff cookies
main_ingredients: whipped cream or cream cheese frosting

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard slice of Golden Biscoff Dream cake, approximately 120 g, as typically served at a café or dessert bar in Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Chocolate layer cake with frosting (SR Legacy #18101) adapted to Biscoff cream cake profile; cross-referenced with USDA Branded Food entries for Lotus Biscoff Spread and Lotus Biscoff cookies
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact entry for Golden Biscoff Dream exists in any approved primary source. Nutrient values are estimated by combining USDA SR Legacy data for layered cream cake (FDC ID 18101) with USDA Branded Food data for Lotus Biscoff Spread (FDC ID 2003571) and Lotus Biscoff cookies (FDC ID 2003570). The resulting composite reflects a typical Biscoff-flavoured cream cake: high in refined carbohydrates, sugar, and saturated fat, with moderate protein from dairy and eggs. Values are per 100 g of finished cake. Confidence is medium due to estimation.

gi_source.title: Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008. Diabetes Care. 2008;31(12):2281-2283. — Sponge cake entry (GI 46) and shortbread/speculoos cookie entry (GI 64) averaged and weighted toward cake-dominant composition; University of Sydney GI Database entry for vanilla cake (GI 42) also referenced.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database; Diabetes Care (peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Golden Biscoff Dream. GI estimated at 55 based on a weighted composite of: (1) University of Sydney GI Database vanilla/sponge cake GI ~42–46; (2) Atkinson et al. 2008 shortbread/speculoos cookie GI ~64; weighted approximately 70% cake base and 30% Biscoff cookie/spread component, yielding an estimated GI of ~52–56. GI set to 55 (upper bound of low category) to be conservative. GL per serving = (55 × 49.0 × 1.20) / 100 = 32.3 g. gi_category is low per validator threshold (GI ≤ 55 = low).

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content (~210 mg/100 g) and high caloric density from saturated fat and sugar
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit portion size to one small slice (≤80 g) and avoid pairing with other high-sodium foods, as the cumulative sodium and saturated fat load can contribute to elevated blood pressure over time.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High sugar content (~32 g/100 g) and high refined carbohydrate load (~49 g/100 g) with moderate GI (~55)
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should treat this cake as an occasional indulgence only; a standard 120 g slice delivers approximately 38 g of sugar and 59 g of total carbohydrate, which will cause a significant postprandial glucose rise and should be accounted for in the daily carbohydrate budget.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High saturated fat (~10.5 g/100 g), trans fat (~0.2 g/100 g), and added sugar contributing to atherogenic lipid profile
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Patients with cardiovascular disease should minimise consumption of this cake due to its high saturated fat and trans fat content, which can raise LDL cholesterol; if consumed, a portion of no more than 60 g is advisable and should be an infrequent exception within a heart-healthy dietary pattern.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Golden Biscoff Dream is a rich, indulgent dessert cake sold at EC Coffee Bar on Carpenter Street in Singapore, featuring the distinctive caramelised spice flavour of Lotus Biscoff (speculoos) cookies and spread. The cake typically consists of a soft sponge or butter cake base layered with Biscoff-infused cream cheese or whipped cream frosting, generously topped with Biscoff cookie butter spread and crushed Biscoff cookies. The flavour profile is deeply sweet and warmly spiced with cinnamon, nutmeg, and ginger notes characteristic of Belgian speculoos biscuits, complemented by the richness of cream and butter. The texture contrasts a moist, tender crumb with a crunchy cookie topping and a silky, spreadable cookie-butter layer. Nutritionally, this cake is energy-dense at approximately 390 kcal per 100 g, with high sugar (~32 g/100 g), high saturated fat (~10.5 g/100 g), and moderate sodium (~210 mg/100 g). It is classified as ultra-processed and is not suitable for regular consumption by individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes, cardiovascular disease, or hypertension. The glycemic index is estimated at 55 (low category), but the glycemic load per standard 120 g serving is approximately 32 g, making it clinically significant for blood glucose management. It is vegetarian and halal-friendly but contains gluten, dairy, and eggs. Priced at SGD 12.40, it is positioned as a premium café dessert in the Singapore food scene.

confidence_reason: No exact entry for Golden Biscoff Dream exists in any approved primary nutritional database. Nutrient values are estimated from USDA FoodData Central data for comparable layered cream cakes and Lotus Biscoff branded products. GI is an analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database entries for sponge cake and speculoos cookies. Confidence is medium rather than low because the component-level USDA data is high quality and the estimation methodology is transparent and defensible.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (layered cream cake, FDC ID 18101) and USDA Branded Food entries for Lotus Biscoff Spread and Lotus Biscoff cookies. No exact approved-source entry exists for this specific product. 2. GI is estimated at 55 (low category per validator threshold ≤55) using a weighted composite of University of Sydney GI Database sponge cake (~42–46) and speculoos cookie (~64) entries; see gi_source for full methodology. 3. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy cake reference entry. 4. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy cake reference entry. 5. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy cake reference entry. 6. vitamin_c_mg is 0.0; confirmed zero as cakes of this type contain no vitamin C-bearing ingredients. 7. trans_fat_g (~0.2 g/100 g) reflects trace naturally occurring trans fats from dairy and butter components; not from partially hydrogenated oils. 8. Halal status is assumed based on the restaurant context (EC Coffee Bar, Singapore) and standard Lotus Biscoff product halal certification; not independently verified for this specific preparation. 9. Price SGD 12.40 preserved from input record. 10. This food is classified under Desserts rather than Mixed to better reflect its primary nature as a dessert cake product.
